current_task_intent: Evaluate whether a candidate public frontier source may influence a longitudinal health or veterinary research-infrastructure hypothesis.
candidate_memory_or_evidence: Preprint evidence that task-conditioned memory admission reduces memory-induced threats compared with similarity-only retrieval.
task_fit_rationale: The source directly addresses memory admission as a trust boundary, but it is bounded to agent benchmarks rather than clinical or veterinary outcomes.
privacy_transformation: No private user or animal data included; fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
transfer_boundaries: personal-agent memory benchmark to biomedical research workflow
human workflow/HCI evidence to veterinary infrastructure design
preprint claim to production gate requirement
synthetic_real_mismatch: Benchmark and fixture data may not reproduce real-world timing, privacy, and longitudinal missingness patterns.
blocked_inferences: does not imply medical diagnosis quality
does not imply veterinary treatment validity
does not permit private memory reuse without consent and provenance checks
revision_reason: Similarity-only evidence retrieval is insufficient for safe scientific memory admission.
falsification_route: Compare reviewer error, leakage rate, and inappropriate cross-domain reuse with and without this gate; falsify if the gate adds no measurable reduction in admitted unsafe evidence.
next_executable_action: Run validator and compare valid and invalid fixtures.
